name: 29 · Turtle Rotate (ROS action + streaming channel)
description:
End-to-end ROS ACTION demo against a turtlesim robot reachable over rosbridge, exercising the `send_action_goal` operation and the streaming-channel feedback path (docs/25). A four-step net rotates the toy turtle to a target orientation and reduces the action's feedback stream:

  Start → teleport (call_service /turtle1/teleport_absolute, turtlesim/srv/TeleportAbsolute, {x:5.5,y:5.5,theta:0.0}) → rotate (send_action_goal /turtle1/rotate_absolute, turtlesim/action/RotateAbsolute, {theta:0.4}, Control/Scatter out channel `feedback`) → fold (Python reducer) → End.

The rotate node declares a Control/Scatter `out` channel `feedback` (max_fanout 1024). Its rosbridge `send_action_goal` op dispatches the goal with `feedback:true`; the ROS backend dedups the DUPLICATED feedback frames rosbridge emits and streams each DISTINCT `feedback { remaining }` as a `scatter_item` control token into the channel (via the in-process `EventStream::scatter_item` path — the same engine `control_emit` seam a Python `with scatter(...)` producer reaches through the IPC `EmitControl`). On the terminal result it stamps a `scatter_close` carrying the count. The compiler's per-channel gather barrier re-orders the items and parks the gathered collection `{ output: [{ remaining }, ...] }` on the channel's gathered place; the downstream `fold` consumer (a plain Python AutomatedStep) drains it off `input.output`. No per-feedback token enters the marking (2 net firings for the whole stream) and the terminal action `delta` rides `stdout_tail` only.

fold sets `feedback_count` (N distinct feedbacks) and `rotation_delta` (the first `remaining` ≈ the requested rotation magnitude); End maps both plus a literal `final_theta = 0.4`.

The two ROS AutomatedSteps are deployed to the `ros_fleet` presence pool (seeded from demos/resources/ros_fleet.json) AND declare the placement Requirement `ros.robot_model == turtlesim` over the `ros` capability type (seeded from demos/capability-types/ros.json). The ROS connection is runner-local — the runner advertises a reachable rosbridge and its executor daemon is configured with the WebSocket URL (`EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL`); there is no workspace resource binding. The `fold` reducer runs on the default inline executor.

LIVE ONLY: this demo can only be RUN after `just dev ros-up`, which builds the turtlesim+rosbridge container, runs it on a slot-aware host port, and enrolls a `ros_fleet` runner advertising `ros.robot_model=turtlesim, ros.ros_distro=jazzy` whose executor points `EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL` at that bridge. The leading teleport resets theta to 0 so a small target (0.4 rad) always yields a real rotation with multiple feedbacks. Keep the target small — a near-2π rotation produces hundreds of feedback frames and a long run.
